name: Granian (Rust-Powered Python HTTP Server)

auth.notes: HTTP server; auth handled by application

performance.notes: Faster than uvicorn in benchmarks; Rust async I/O via tokio; RSGI protocol

agent_readiness.notes: Rust-powered Python ASGI/WSGI server. granian --interface asgi app:app --host 0.0.0.0 --port 8000. Faster than uvicorn in most benchmarks (Rust tokio runtime). ASGI, WSGI, RSGI (Rust Server Gateway Interface) support. HTTP/1, HTTP/2, WebSockets. Works with FastAPI, Starlette, Django, Flask. Multi-process via --workers. TLS support. No uvloop dependency needed (Rust handles async I/O). Growing adoption as uvicorn alternative.

gotchas: Less battle-tested than uvicorn in production
Some ASGI edge cases may differ from uvicorn behavior
RSGI interface only supported by Emmett framework
best_when: High-performance Python ASGI applications needing faster server
FastAPI/Starlette apps wanting uvicorn performance boost
avoid_when: Production systems where uvicorn's track record is preferred
Environments where binary wheels aren't available
summary: Granian is a Rust-powered Python ASGI/WSGI server outperforming uvicorn in benchmarks. granian --interface asgi app:app. HTTP/1, HTTP/2, WebSockets. Works with FastAPI, Django, Flask. No uvloop needed — Rust tokio handles async I/O. Growing uvicorn alternative for performance-critical deployments. Less battle-tested than uvicorn but faster in benchmarks.
